summary: Added a Chapter Command Log inside the selected Path Map Chapter Archive record deck so each chapter shows the current suggested local draft, staged outbox items, browser-local operator history, and chapter context in one compact handoff timeline.

queries: command center activity log UX pattern operations dashboard timeline handoff
audit trail dashboard UX activity log command center design pattern
incident response handoff timeline tasks communication log dashboard UX
pattern_notes: Command-center dashboards need fast status recognition plus a clear path to the next action.
Handoff timelines are more useful when they combine current state, queued work, and recent operator actions.
Audit-style activity logs should be compact, sequential, and scoped to the thing the operator is inspecting.

ui_contract: Render Chapter Command Log inside the selected Chapter Archive record deck.
Show the current suggested local command for the selected lane.
Show staged outbox and browser-local history rows scoped to the lane when present.
Always include selected chapter context so fresh browsers still have a useful handoff trail.
Expose Q, COM, and TRL actions without sending messages or starting workers.
Keep the log responsive with no clipped labels or horizontal overflow.

red_green.red: pytest tests\test_agent_company_atlas_path_chapter_command_log.py -q failed before frontend wiring on missing function pathChapterCommandLogRecords.

browser_green_route.evidence: Rendered Chapter Command Log for the 06/15 chapter with five entries: suggested gated draft, queued outbox drafts, local operator history, and selected chapter context. Actions rendered as OK/COM/TRL after the fresh script cache loaded.

local_queue_action.evidence: The command-log Q/OK button resolved to exactly one element and clicked successfully through the existing browser-local lane staging handler. The detail panel now refreshes after staging so queued lanes can show OK state.

responsive.evidence: All tested viewports rendered one command log with five entries, OK/COM/TRL actions, no horizontal overflow, no bad rectangles, no clipped command text, and no console errors.
